features.featureTitle: Completely replace the camera toggle handler in `frontend/src/components/room/CameraPreview.tsx`. The new implementation must: (1) attach an onClick handler directly on the toggle button that — synchronously and without any awaited pre-checks — calls `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true, audio: false })` as the very first asynchronous operation, ensuring the browser native permission popup is triggered; (2) stop all tracks on any existing stream before requesting a new one; (3) on success, assign the stream to the video element ref (`videoRef.current.srcObject = stream`) and call `videoRef.current.play()`; (4) on failure, set an error state and render the message 'Camera access was denied. Please allow camera access when the browser prompt appears and try again.'; (5) apply a permanent inline CSS blur filter (`filter: 'blur(12px) brightness(0.8) contrast(1.2)'`) to the `<video>` element at all times — never remove or toggle it; (6) stop all tracks and nullify the stream on component unmount. Strip out every prior attempt's code: no `enumerateDevices`, no device-ID constraints, no resolution constraints, no feature-detection guards, no try/catch blocks wrapping the `getUserMedia` call itself before the call is made.
